residual_risks.description: Test helper duplication: createMockFileHandle and createMockDirectoryHandle are defined identically at both the describe('scanDirectory') scope (lines 104-134) and inside the nested describe('maxDepth') scope (lines 198-228). Not a standards violation, but minor maintenance burden if the mock shape changes.

The depth calculation (basePath.split('/').length) treats each path segment as one depth level. This is correct for the File System Access API which always uses '/' separators, but could surprise a future maintainer unfamiliar with the API's path conventions.

testing_gaps.description: No test covering the interaction between maxDepth and includeImages: false (e.g., maxDepth:0 with includeImages:false should still yield videos/PDFs at root but skip subdirectory files). Current tests only exercise includeImages: true for maxDepth scenarios.

No test verifying that maxDepth: 0 with recursive pass still finds files in subdirectories when maxDepth is unset (i.e., the two-pass pattern in courseImport.ts is indirectly tested, but the fallback behavior could use explicit coverage).
